column2: Out and Proud // LGBTQ+ Films Ranked
column5:
Identity is one of the most fascinating aspects to human life and I constantly think about it, both in myself and those close to me. One of the biggest parts of my personal and artistic identity is my sexual orientation. As I've both grown as a film buff and learned more about who I'm attracted to, I've been introduced to a broad range of LGBTQ+ media that has impacted me in some way. This representation is a big goal in my own work and I cannot express enough how important this is to my own life.

With this list, I'm gonna try to do my best at ranking every queer-coded film I've given a rating to // watched since 2019. Subject to change.

A weird choice for sure, but director Sergei Eisenstein was openly queer and the film features several scenes of shirtless men so I think it counts.

8.3 / 10

column2: The Passion of Joan of Arc
column5:
Joan of Arc's gender and sexual identities have been debated for decades upon decades. I don't remember if that has any tie-in to this movie, but until I rewatch it, I'll keep it on this list.

7.3 / 10
